evals.prompt:
Review my SQL Server encryption posture. Here is the DMV output:

sys.databases (is_encrypted column):
SalesDB: 0
HRPayroll: 0
ArchiveDB: 0
tempdb: 0

sys.dm_database_encryption_keys: (no rows)

master.sys.certificates:
BackupCert | expiry_date: 2024-01-01 | pvt_key_encryption_type_desc: ENCRYPTED_BY_MASTER_KEY
TLSCert    | expiry_date: 2027-01-01 | pvt_key_encryption_type_desc: ENCRYPTED_BY_MASTER_KEY

sys.symmetric_keys (FinanceDB):
PaymentKey | TRIPLE_DES_3KEY | created: 2018-03-01 | modified: 2018-03-01
TokenKey   | RC4_128        | created: 2017-11-15 | modified: 2017-11-15

msdb.dbo.backupset (last 30 days):
SalesDB   | key_algorithm: NULL
HRPayroll | key_algorithm: NULL
ArchiveDB | key_algorithm: NULL

sys.dm_exec_connections:
session 42 | client_net_address: 10.0.0.55 | encrypt_option: FALSE
session 43 | client_net_address: 10.0.0.60 | encrypt_option: FALSE

sys.databases (is_master_key_encrypted_by_server):
SalesDB: 0
HRPayroll: 1

No SQL Server Audit configured.
evals.expected_output: Analysis should identify: (1) A1 - all user databases lack TDE (Critical for SalesDB/HRPayroll given naming), (2) A17 - TRIPLE_DES_3KEY and RC4_128 symmetric keys (Critical), (3) A22 - all backups unencrypted (Critical), (4) A4 - BackupCert expired 2024-01-01, (5) A26/A27 - unencrypted remote connections, (6) A45 - SalesDB DMK not encrypted by SMK, (7) A20/A41 - symmetric keys never rotated (7-9 years old), (8) A56 - no audit configured. Should include Fix Plan with prioritized remediation steps. Should NOT flag HRPayroll DMK (is_master_key_encrypted_by_server = 1 is correct).

evals.prompt:
Run an encryption review. Here is what I have:

sys.dm_database_encryption_keys:
CustomerDB | ENCRYPTED | 100% | key_algorithm: AES_256 | encryptor_thumbprint: 0xABCD

master.sys.certificates:
TDECert2023 | expiry_date: 2025-08-01 | pvt_key_encryption_type_desc: ENCRYPTED_BY_MASTER_KEY | issuer_name = subject (self-signed)
TDECert2023 appears twice (same subject 'CN=TDECert2023' for two different certificate_id values)

sys.sensitivity_classifications (CustomerDB):
CustomerDB.dbo.Customer.email_address | LABEL: Personal | INFORMATION_TYPE: Contact | no column_encryption_key_id
CustomerDB.dbo.Customer.national_id   | LABEL: Highly Confidential | INFORMATION_TYPE: National ID | no column_encryption_key_id
CustomerDB.dbo.Payment.card_number    | LABEL: Highly Confidential | INFORMATION_TYPE: Banking | no column_encryption_key_id

msdb.dbo.backupset (last 30 days):
CustomerDB | key_algorithm: AES_128 | encryptor_thumbprint: 0xABCD

sys.dm_exec_connections:
All connections show encrypt_option: TRUE

sys.configurations:
column encryption enclave type: 0

SQL Agent jobs:
Job 'Backup CustomerDB' runs BACKUP with ENCRYPTION — exists and ran 2026-06-05
No 'BACKUP CERTIFICATE' job found
No 'BACKUP MASTER KEY' job found
evals.expected_output: Analysis should identify: (1) A4 - TDECert2023 expiry 2025-08-01 is already past (about 10 months expired), (2) A38 - duplicate subject/CN on TDECert2023 (two certs with same subject), (3) A36 - self-signed TDE certificate in production, (4) A53 - sensitivity-classified columns (national_id, card_number rated Highly Confidential) lack column encryption, (5) A24 - backup encryption using AES_128 (recommend AES_256), (6) A23 - backup cert not backed up despite backups being encrypted (Critical - encrypted backups with unbackedup cert), (7) A44 - no master key backup, (8) A3 - TDE cert not backed up. Should NOT flag A26/A27 (all connections encrypted). Should NOT flag A1 (CustomerDB is encrypted). Should NOT flag A22 (backups are encrypted, just weak algorithm). Fix plan should prioritize cert rotation and backup operations.

evals.prompt:
Please review my Always Encrypted and key hierarchy setup:

sys.column_master_keys:
CMK_Production | key_store_provider_name: MSSQL_CERTIFICATE_STORE | key_path: CurrentUser/My/... | create_date: 2021-03-10

sys.column_encryption_keys:
CEK_CustomerPII | create_date: 2021-03-10 (single entry in sys.column_encryption_key_values — never rotated)

sys.columns (CustomerDB):
dbo.Customer.ssn          | encryption_type: 2 (RANDOMIZED) | encryption_algorithm: AEAD_AES_256_CBC_HMAC_SHA_256
dbo.Customer.first_name   | encryption_type: 1 (DETERMINISTIC) | encryption_algorithm: AEAD_AES_256_CBC_HMAC_SHA_256
dbo.Customer.last_name    | encryption_type: 1 (DETERMINISTIC) | encryption_algorithm: AEAD_AES_256_CBC_HMAC_SHA_256
dbo.Payment.account_number| encryption_type: 1 (DETERMINISTIC) | encryption_algorithm: AEAD_AES_256_CBC_HMAC_SHA_256

sys.configurations:
column encryption enclave type: 0 (no enclave)

sys.asymmetric_keys:
LegacySignKey | key_length: 1024 | create_date: 2015-06-01

sys.symmetric_keys:
ArchiveKey | AES_256 | create_date: 2016-01-10 | modify_date: 2016-01-10

sys.key_encryptions (ArchiveKey):
crypt_type_desc: ENCRYPTION_BY_PASSWORD (only entry)

sys.database_permissions:
grantee: AppServiceAccount | object: CEK_CustomerPII | permission_name: CONTROL
evals.expected_output: Analysis should identify: (1) A10 - dbo.Customer.ssn uses RANDOMIZED encryption but no secure enclave configured — WHERE/range queries on ssn will fail, (2) A9 - first_name and last_name use DETERMINISTIC encryption; names are not typically used as join/filter keys, suggesting RANDOMIZED would be more privacy-preserving (Info), (3) A13 - CMK stored in Windows Certificate Store (MSSQL_CERTIFICATE_STORE) rather than AKV or HSM (Warning), (4) A15/A16 - CMK_Production is 5+ years old (created 2021-03-10), CEK never rotated since creation — both overdue for rotation, (5) A39 - LegacySignKey is RSA_1024 (NIST deprecated since 2010) — Warning/upgrade to RSA_2048, (6) A41/A20 - ArchiveKey symmetric key never rotated since 2016 (10 years), (7) A19 - ArchiveKey protected by password only (no certificate in hierarchy), (8) A40 - AppServiceAccount has CONTROL permission on CEK_CustomerPII — should be REFERENCES only. Should recommend AKV migration for CMK, RSA_2048 replacement for LegacySignKey, and CEK rotation plan.

evals.prompt:
Azure SQL Database review — we have a PCI-compliant Azure SQL DB. Review encryption posture:

sys.dm_database_encryption_keys:
PaymentDB | ENCRYPTED | 100% | encryptor_type: SERVICE_MANAGED | key_algorithm: AES_256

Azure Portal shows:
- TDE protector: Service-managed
- Infrastructure encryption: OFF
- Key Vault region: <none — service-managed>
- Double encryption not enabled

sys.sensitivity_classifications (PaymentDB):
PaymentDB.dbo.Cardholder.card_number | LABEL: Highly Confidential | INFORMATION_TYPE: Banking | no column_encryption_key_id
PaymentDB.dbo.Cardholder.cvv | LABEL: Highly Confidential | INFORMATION_TYPE: Credentials | no column_encryption_key_id

sys.column_master_keys / sys.column_encryption_keys: (0 rows — Always Encrypted not deployed)

sys.dm_exec_connections: all connections show encrypt_option: TRUE (Azure enforces TLS)

sys.server_audits: audit to Azure Blob Storage — storage account encryption: service-managed keys (not CMK)

sys.configurations: column encryption enclave type: 0
evals.expected_output: Analysis should identify: (1) A51 - service-managed TDE in a PCI-regulated environment — PCI-DSS Requirement 3.6 expects customer-managed keys for data sovereignty, (2) A78 - double encryption not enabled (Info for PCI compliance — defense-in-depth), (3) A53 - sensitivity-classified columns (card_number rated Highly Confidential / Banking, cvv rated Credentials) lack column encryption (Warning — PCI-DSS Req 3.4.1), (4) A80 - audit logs not encrypted with customer-managed keys (Info for PCI Req 10), (5) A14 - sensitive-pattern card columns without AE. Should note that Azure TLS enforcement is automatic (no A26/A27 flags). Should recommend BYOK migration, infrastructure encryption, AE for cardholder data.

evals.prompt:
SQL Server 2022 with Ledger enabled — review encryption posture:

AuditDB | is_ledger_on: 0 | is_encrypted: 1

AuditDB | ENCRYPTED | 100% | key_algorithm: AES_256 | encryptor_thumbprint: 0xBBB

sys.database_ledger_configurations:

SQL Agent jobs:
No job for sp_generate_database_ledger_digest
No job for sp_verify_database_ledger
No job for BACKUP CERTIFICATE

Azure Storage / ACL digest endpoint: not configured

sys.dm_exec_connections:
All connections show encrypt_option: TRUE, auth_scheme: KERBEROS

SChannel registry (via xp_regread):
TLS 1.0 Server Enabled: 1
TLS 1.1 Server Enabled: 0
TLS 1.2 Server Enabled: 1
Cipher suites include: RC4 128/128

SQL Server 2022 on Windows Server 2022

No Kerberos armoring GPO applied
evals.expected_output: Analysis should identify: (1) A74 - Ledger database digest not configured for automatic storage (Warning — no external verification possible), (2) A76 - Ledger verification not scheduled (Warning — tampering may go undetected), (3) A3 - no certificate backup job, (4) A73 - AuditDB would benefit from Ledger (compliance-named database, no ledger), (5) A75 - SHA-256 confirmed as ledger hash (pass, Info), (6) A57 - TLS 1.0 enabled at OS level (Warning), (7) A58 - RC4 cipher suite enabled (Warning), (8) A59 - TLS 1.3 not enforced even though SQL 2022 on Win 2022+ (Info), (9) A62 - Named Pipes enabled in production (Warning), (10) A61 - Kerberos armoring not enforced (Info). Should recommend digest storage configuration, TLS hardening, and ledger expansion.

evals.prompt:
Multi-instance production deployment — encryption review:

Instance PROD-SQL-01 (primary):
- 3 databases: SalesDB (TDE: AES_256, cert: TDECertShared), HRDB (TDE: AES_256, cert: TDECertShared), FinanceDB (TDE: AES_256, cert: TDECertShared)
- All 3 databases share cert TDECertShared
- TDECertShared expiry: 2028-12-31

TLS config:
- SQL Server Configuration Manager shows ForceEncryption: OFF
- Certificate bound: self-signed (issuer = subject = CN=PROD-SQL-01)
- All application connection strings use TrustServerCertificate=true
- 5 remote sessions show encrypt_option: FALSE

Key hierarchy:
- DMK backed up: No BACKUP MASTER KEY job
- SMK backed up: No BACKUP SERVICE MASTER KEY job
- Certificate backup: No BACKUP CERTIFICATE job (for TDECertShared or any other cert)

Backups:
- All 3 databases backed up with ENCRYPTION (AES_256, cert: BackupCert)
- BackupCert expiry: 2026-01-15 (expired 5 months ago, today is June 2026)

Audit:
- No SQL Server Audit configured

No sensitivity classifications applied
No Always Encrypted columns
No CLE keys
evals.expected_output: Analysis should identify: (1) A6 - 3 databases share single TDE certificate (Warning — single point of compromise/rotation), (2) A3 - TDECertShared not backed up (Critical), (3) A26 - ForceEncryption OFF, (4) A27 - 5 active remote unencrypted sessions, (5) A28 - self-signed TLS cert (Info for dev, Warning for prod), (6) A29 - TrustServerCertificate=TRUE in use, (7) A44 - DMK not backed up, (8) A47 - SMK not backed up, (9) A37 - no certificate backup for any cert, (10) A25 - BackupCert expired 5 months ago, (11) A56 - no audit, (12) A54/A53 - no sensitivity classifications. Should recommend dedicated TDE certs per DB, TLS cert from CA, certificate backup jobs.

evals.expected_output: Analysis should identify: (1) A1 - LegacyAppDB not TDE-encrypted (Warning, database age 6+ years), (2) A20/A41 - CustomerDataKey never rotated since 2020 (6 years), SessionTokenKey never rotated since 2019 (7 years — Warning), (3) A19 - SessionTokenKey protected by password only, (4) A35 - DataProtectionCert signed with SHA1 (deprecated), (5) A45/A46 - DMK not protected by SMK — manual OPEN MASTER KEY required after every restart (Warning), (6) A44 - DMK not backed up, (7) A37 - DataProtectionCert not backed up, (8) A22 - backups unencrypted, (9) A72 - log backups unencrypted despite being in full recovery (if applicable). Should prioritize AGEndpointCert rotation, EKM provider re-enablement, and TLS hardening.

SSISDB and non-SMK DMK scenario — review encryption posture:

master.sys.master_key_passwords:
(0 rows — no passwords registered for any database)

(0 rows — no user credentials)

SQL Server ERRORLOG (last startup 2026-06-01):

(no FIPS compliance message)

session 15 | client_net_address: 192.168.5.33 | encrypt_option: TRUE | auth_scheme: KERBEROS

SQL Agent jobs: None named with key/cert/dmk/smk patterns
evals.expected_output: Analysis should identify: (1) A82 - SSISDB exists with is_master_key_encrypted_by_server=0 AND no entry in sys.master_key_passwords (Critical — SSIS operations fail on restart), (2) A81 - WarehouseDB also has is_master_key_encrypted_by_server=0 with no registered password (Warning), (3) A85 - WarehouseDB was restored from PROD-SQL01 (restore history present) and has no password registration on this instance (Warning), (4) A84 - both SSISDB and WarehouseDB have no auto-open path (no SMK protection, no password registered, no startup job), (5) A27 - session 12 is a remote connection without TLS encryption, (6) A106 - session 12 is authenticating via NTLM (Info), (7) A28 - self-generated TLS certificate at startup (Warning), (8) A97 - no key management jobs (Info), (9) A1 - AnalyticsDB is not encrypted with TDE (Warning). Should recommend: sp_control_dbmasterkey_password for SSISDB (A82 is Critical — fix immediately), register WarehouseDB, investigate the ForceEncryption gap. Should NOT flag AnalyticsDB DMK (is_master_key_encrypted_by_server=1 is correct).

evals.prompt:
Dynamic data masking and permission pattern review:

Database: RetailDB

sys.masked_columns:
dbo.Customer.CreditCardNumber | masking_function: partial(0,'XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-',4) | column_encryption_key_id: NULL
dbo.Customer.SSN | masking_function: default() | column_encryption_key_id: NULL
dbo.Employee.Salary | masking_function: random(1,9999) | column_encryption_key_id: NULL

sys.database_permissions (UNMASK grants):
grantee: db_datareader (role with 45 members) | permission_name: UNMASK
grantee: ReportingUser | permission_name: UNMASK

sys.security_policies:
CustomerRowFilter | predicate on dbo.Customer WHERE ManagerId = USER_ID()

sys.columns (dbo.Customer.CustomerID_AE):
column_encryption_key_id: 3 | encryption_type_desc: DETERMINISTIC

sys.security_policies (predicate function definition):
fn_CustomerFilter references dbo.Customer.CustomerID_AE (AE column)

sys.database_permissions:
EncryptionCert | CONTROL | grantee: AppServiceUser

sys.sensitivity_classifications:
dbo.Customer.CreditCardNumber | information_type: Banking | rank: HIGH | column_encryption_key_id: NULL
dbo.Customer.SSN | information_type: National ID | rank: HIGH | column_encryption_key_id: NULL
evals.expected_output: Analysis should identify: (1) A87 - CreditCardNumber and SSN are masked but not encrypted despite sensitivity classifications (Warning — DDM is not encryption; sysadmin sees real data), (2) A88 - UNMASK granted to db_datareader role with 45 members (Warning — effectively removes masking for 45 users), (3) A90 - RLS predicate function fn_CustomerFilter references the AE-encrypted CustomerID_AE column (Warning — predicate receives ciphertext, not plaintext; will not filter correctly), (4) A89 - CONTROL permission on EncryptionCert granted to AppServiceUser (Warning — CONTROL allows DROP/ALTER on the cert), (5) A53 - Sensitivity-classified columns (Banking, National ID, rank HIGH) lack column encryption despite classification (Warning). Should NOT flag ReportingUser UNMASK (individual user grant is acceptable). Should recommend AE/CLE for CreditCardNumber and SSN, revoke UNMASK from db_datareader role, redesign RLS for AE columns to use application-layer filtering.

evals.prompt:
Plan cache exposure, job step password, and AKV issues — review:

Instance: PROD-AZURE-SQL01 (Azure SQL Managed Instance)

sys.dm_exec_query_stats + sys.dm_exec_sql_text:
-- Query in plan cache (full text visible):
-- OPEN SYMMETRIC KEY PaymentKey DECRYPTION BY PASSWORD = 'Prod$ecret99'
-- SELECT DECRYPTBYKEY(EncryptedPAN) FROM dbo.Payments

msdb.dbo.sysjobsteps:
Job: 'Daily Payment Encryption' | Step: 'Open Key and Encrypt' |
command: 'OPEN SYMMETRIC KEY PaymentKey DECRYPTION BY PASSWORD = ''Prod$ecret99''; UPDATE dbo.Payments...'

Azure Key Vault (vault: prod-secrets-kv):
enableSoftDelete: false
enablePurgeProtection: false

TDE on PaymentDB (sys.dm_database_encryption_keys):
encryptor_type: ASYMMETRIC_KEY (AKV BYOK)
key_algorithm: AES_256

Managed Instance managed identity:
Permissions on prod-secrets-kv: wrapKey, get (missing: unwrapKey)

msdb.dbo.backupset (last 365 days, PaymentDB):
All encrypted: key_algorithm: AES_256

msdb.dbo.restorehistory (PaymentDB):
Last restore: 2024-11-01 (over 1 year ago)
evals.expected_output: Analysis should identify: (1) A100 - Plan cache contains OPEN SYMMETRIC KEY with literal password 'Prod$ecret99' (Critical — visible to all VIEW SERVER STATE holders), (2) A99 - SQL Agent job step 'Daily Payment Encryption' contains hardcoded OPEN SYMMETRIC KEY password (Critical — stored in MSDB near-plaintext), (3) A101 - AKV vault prod-secrets-kv has enableSoftDelete=false AND enablePurgeProtection=false (Critical — AKV BYOK TDE key can be permanently deleted), (4) A112 - Managed Instance managed identity missing 'unwrapKey' permission on AKV (Critical — DEK cannot be re-wrapped after key rotation; failover groups at risk), (5) A102 - Last restore test was 2024-11-01 (over 1 year ago) for encrypted PaymentDB (Warning). Should recommend: immediate DBCC FREEPROCCACHE to evict exposed plan, migrate symmetric key to certificate-based protection, enable AKV soft-delete + purge protection, add unwrapKey permission to managed identity. Should NOT flag TDE algorithm (AES_256 is correct). Critical items should be top priority.
